title: Europe Faces Record-Breaking Heatwave as Temperatures Soar
subtitle: Countries across the continent report all-time high temperatures and rising death tolls as extreme heat moves east.

summary.en: Europe is currently experiencing an intense and dangerous heatwave that has set new temperature records in several countries. Germany, Poland, and the Czech Republic have all reported temperatures exceeding 40 degrees Celsius. Health officials are concerned about the rising number of deaths, particularly among older people. Authorities have issued warnings and taken emergency measures to help residents stay cool. Experts link this extreme weather to the ongoing effects of climate change.
summary.zh: 歐洲目前正經歷一波強烈且危險的熱浪，多個國家創下新的氣溫紀錄。德國、波蘭與捷克皆通報氣溫超過攝氏四十度。衛生官員對於死亡人數攀升感到憂心，特別是高齡族群。當局已發布警報並採取緊急措施協助居民降溫。專家將此極端天氣歸因於氣候變遷持續造成的影響。

paragraphs.en: A severe heatwave is hitting Europe, with countries in the east now facing the same dangerous conditions that previously affected the west. According to BBC News, temperature records were broken across the continent as the extreme heat moved eastward. Germany, Poland, and the Czech Republic all saw temperatures climb above 40 degrees Celsius over the weekend, creating difficult and life-threatening situations for millions of people.
paragraphs.zh: 一波嚴重的熱浪正席捲歐洲，東歐國家目前正面臨先前影響西歐的危險氣候狀況。根據英國廣播公司新聞（BBC News）報導，隨著極端高溫向東移動，歐洲大陸各地的氣溫紀錄紛紛被打破。德國、波蘭與捷克在週末期間氣溫皆攀升至攝氏四十度以上，為數百萬民眾帶來艱困且危及生命的處境。

paragraphs.en: In Germany, the national weather service reported a new all-time high of 41.7 degrees Celsius in the town of Coschen, near the Polish border. This was the third day in a row that the country set a new heat record. Meanwhile, The Guardian reported that the Czech Republic reached an even higher temperature of 41.9 degrees Celsius in the village of Doksany. Poland also broke its own historical record, with temperatures in the town of Slubice reaching 40.5 degrees Celsius, surpassing a record that had stood for over a century.
paragraphs.zh: 在德國，國家氣象局通報位於波蘭邊境附近的科申鎮（Coschen）創下攝氏四十一點七度的歷史新高。這是該國連續第三天刷新高溫紀錄。與此同時，《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，捷克的多克薩尼村（Doksany）氣溫甚至達到更高的攝氏四十一點九度。波蘭也打破了自身的歷史紀錄，斯武比采鎮（Slubice）氣溫來到攝氏四十點五度，超越了維持超過一個世紀的紀錄。

paragraphs.en: As the heat spreads, the human cost is becoming clearer. BBC News reports that more than 1,300 excess deaths—deaths beyond what is usually expected—have been linked to the high temperatures since June 21. In France, officials reported at least 1,000 more deaths than normal since the heatwave began. The French national public health agency noted that the sharpest increase in fatalities involved people dying at home, with the majority being over the age of 65. Emergency doctors have warned that the final death toll could be much higher, as some people living alone may not be discovered for several days.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著熱浪蔓延，人命損失也愈發明顯。BBC新聞報導指出，自六月二十一日以來，已有超過一千三百起超額死亡（即超出平時預期的死亡人數）與高溫有關。在法國，官員通報自熱浪開始以來，死亡人數比平時至少多出一千人。法國國家公共衛生署指出，死亡人數激增的情況主要發生在居家死亡者身上，且多數為六十五歲以上的長者。急診醫師警告，最終死亡人數可能會更高，因為部分獨居者可能在數日後才被發現。

paragraphs.en: This extreme weather is often called a "heat dome," a weather pattern that traps hot air over a region like a lid on a pot. The impact on daily life has been significant. In Germany, The Guardian reported that firefighters struggled to manage forest fires in areas where old ammunition from the Second World War was buried in the ground. The heat caused some of these old explosives to go off, forcing hundreds of people to leave their homes for safety. In Berlin, police used water cannons to help residents and tourists cool down, while the national rail operator advised people to avoid any travel that was not strictly necessary.
paragraphs.zh: 這種極端天氣常被稱為「熱穹」（heat dome），這是一種將熱空氣困在區域上方，如同鍋蓋般的氣候型態。這對日常生活造成了重大影響。在德國，《衛報》報導消防人員難以控制森林火災，因為該地區地底下埋有第二次世界大戰時期的舊彈藥。高溫導致部分舊炸藥引爆，迫使數百人撤離家園以策安全。在柏林，警方使用水砲協助居民與遊客降溫，國家鐵路營運商則建議民眾避免非必要的旅行。

paragraphs.en: Authorities across Europe have taken urgent steps to protect public health. In France, officials banned the consumption of alcohol in public and cancelled a pride march to reduce the pressure on emergency services. In Poland, the government sent text messages to citizens, urging them to stay out of the sun, avoid heavy physical activity, and drink plenty of water. Many cities have also set up "water curtains"—sprinklers that create a fine mist—to help people stay cool in public spaces.
paragraphs.zh: 歐洲各地的當局已採取緊急措施以維護公共衛生。在法國，官員禁止在公共場所飲酒並取消了一場同志遊行，以減輕緊急服務單位的壓力。在波蘭，政府向公民發送簡訊，敦促民眾避免曝曬於陽光下、避免劇烈體能活動並多補充水分。許多城市也設置了「水簾」——即噴灑細緻水霧的灑水裝置——以協助民眾在公共空間保持涼爽。

paragraphs.en: Experts emphasize that these events are becoming more common due to climate change. According to BBC News, Europe is warming at twice the global average, and heatwaves that used to happen once in a generation are now occurring almost every year. Officials are now calling for countries to create better "heat health action plans"—official strategies to prepare for and respond to extreme heat—to protect citizens in the future.
paragraphs.zh: 專家強調，由於氣候變遷，這類事件正變得愈發頻繁。根據BBC新聞報導，歐洲的暖化速度是全球平均的兩倍，過去世代才出現一次的熱浪，如今幾乎每年都在發生。官員們正呼籲各國制定更完善的「高溫健康行動計畫」（即為因應極端高溫而準備的官方策略），以在未來保護公民。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the situation remains serious. While some areas may see relief through storms, the heat continues to put a heavy burden on hospitals and emergency services. As The Guardian noted, the French prime minister stated that these services will remain under pressure for the coming days. For now, the primary focus for authorities remains the safety of the most vulnerable, especially those who are isolated or living in crowded urban areas. The combination of record-breaking temperatures and the physical strain on the body serves as a stark reminder of the challenges posed by a warming planet.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，情勢依然嚴峻。儘管部分地區可能會因暴風雨而獲得緩解，但高溫持續對醫院與緊急服務單位造成沉重負擔。《衛報》指出，法國總理表示這些服務單位在未來幾天內仍將承受壓力。目前，當局的首要任務仍是確保最脆弱族群的安全，特別是那些孤立無援或居住在擁擠都會區的民眾。破紀錄的高溫與對身體造成的生理壓力，嚴正提醒了我們全球暖化所帶來的挑戰。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To have remained in a particular state or position for a period of time.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「站立」，這裡指紀錄維持了多久。文中：surpassing a record that had stood for over a century.
常見作「離開」或「變質」，這裡指爆炸。文中：The heat caused some of these old explosives to go off, forcing hundreds of people to leave their homes for safety.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A heavy load or a difficult responsibility.
keyTerms.noteZh: 雖然是名詞，但常與抽象的壓力連用，容易被誤解為實體重量。文中：the heat continues to put a heavy burden on hospitals and emergency services.

sources.name: BBC News
sources.title: Europe's heatwave linked to 1,300 deaths, WHO says, as Germany hits record 41.7C

sources.title: Europe heat wave: Over 1,300 excess deaths, WHO says

previousArticles.title: Europe Faces Dangerous New Highs as Record Heatwave Continues
previousArticles.summaryEn: A severe heatwave is causing record-breaking temperatures across Europe, leading to dangerous conditions for millions. Countries like the UK, France, and Spain are seeing their highest temperatures in decades. Authorities have issued red alerts and taken emergency measures to protect public health. The extreme heat has also forced the shutdown of nuclear reactors and caused significant disruption to transport. Scientists warn that these events are becoming more frequent due to climate change.

previousArticles.title: Europe Faces Dangerous Heatwave as Records Continue to Fall
previousArticles.summaryEn: A severe heatwave is currently affecting many European countries, leading to record-breaking temperatures. Health services are struggling to manage a high number of emergencies as people suffer from the extreme heat. Several countries have issued red alerts, which are the highest level of warning for dangerous weather. Authorities are taking emergency steps, including closing nuclear reactors and banning public alcohol consumption in Paris. Scientists and officials warn that these conditions are becoming increasingly common and dangerous for the public.

previousArticles.title: Europe Faces Dangerous New Highs as Heatwave Continues
previousArticles.summaryEn: A severe heatwave is currently affecting millions of people across Europe, leading to record-breaking temperatures in countries like the UK, France, and Spain. Experts warn that human-induced climate change is making these events more frequent and dangerous. Governments are responding with emergency plans to keep citizens safe. Schools, hospitals, and transport networks are struggling to handle the extreme heat. The World Health Organization has warned that these temperatures are putting lives at risk.

quiz.question: According to the article, what specific danger complicated the efforts of German firefighters during the heatwave?

options.A: The lack of available water in urban areas.
options.B: The risk of old, buried explosives detonating due to the heat.
options.C: The rapid spread of fire caused by the extreme wind.
options.D: The failure of modern communication systems used by emergency services.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第四段，德國消防員在處理森林火災時遇到困難，原因在於地底下埋有第二次世界大戰時期的舊彈藥，高溫導致這些爆炸物意外引爆。選項 A 並未提及缺水問題；選項 C 文中未提及風力因素；選項 D 關於通訊系統的描述與事實不符。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might the final death toll in France be higher than the current official count?

options.A: Because the government is intentionally hiding the true number of fatalities.
options.B: Because many people are refusing to go to hospitals for treatment.
options.C: Because isolated individuals may die at home and remain undiscovered for some time.
options.D: Because the heatwave is expected to last for several more months.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第三段，急診醫生警告最終死亡人數可能更高，因為有些獨居者可能在家中死亡後，需要幾天時間才被發現。選項 A 屬於無根據的猜測；選項 B 文中並未提到民眾拒絕就醫；選項 D 文中僅提到未來幾天壓力仍大，並未預測持續數月。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the author mean by describing the situation as a 'stark reminder'?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在文中 'stark' 一詞意指「嚴峻的」、「明顯且令人不悅的」。作者用此詞來強調氣候變遷帶來的挑戰是現實且嚴重的，不容忽視。選項 A 與 stark 的意思相反；選項 C 與 D 則完全扭曲了該詞在文中的警示語氣。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article?

options.A: European countries are competing to see which can record the highest temperature.
options.B: The heatwave is an isolated event caused by a temporary weather anomaly.
options.C: Extreme heatwaves are becoming more frequent in Europe, necessitating better preparedness and action.
options.D: The most effective way to combat heatwaves is to cancel all public events and gatherings.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章整體探討了歐洲熱浪的現況、帶來的生命與社會威脅，並在第六段明確指出這些極端天氣因氣候變遷變得更頻繁，呼籲各國建立「熱健康行動計畫」。選項 A 過於片面；選項 B 與文中「變得更常見」的論點衝突；選項 D 僅是防疫手段之一，非文章主旨。
